content.full_text:
October 19, 1990

Mr. David Ginsborg  
Campaign Manager  
1150 N. 1st Street  
Suite 170  
San Jose, CA 95112

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-620

Dear Mr. Ginsborg:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on behalf of Peter Carrillo, a candidate for San Jose City Council, concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1

Your letter, dated September 21, 1990, correctly summarizes my advice that the Act does not prohibit an entertainment club from charging an additional fee for entertainment provided on a specific night to benefit a candidate. Persons attending the event will be aware that the additional fee will be contributed to the candidate. If the club delivers the contributions to the candidate, it must identify itself as an intermediary. Contributions aggregating less than $100 in a calendar year from a single source are reported on Schedule A, line 2 (contributions of less than $100-not itemized) of the Form 420 or 490. A candidate's election committee must file the Form 490. A ballot measure committee controlled by a candidate files on the Form 420.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant
